name: Ceramic Matrix Composites CMCs Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Ceramic Matrix Composites CMCs. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for CMC surface contaminant removal without substrate damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength providing optimal absorption balance for CMC ceramic phases and contaminants

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter optimized for precision cleaning of CMC surface features

repetitionRate.description: High repetition rate for efficient area coverage while maintaining thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence level sufficient for contaminant ablation while below CMC damage threshold

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient contaminant ablation with minimal thermal diffusion into CMC substrate

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform contaminant removal across CMC surfaces

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for thorough contaminant removal with process control

overlapRatio.description: Beam overlap percentage ensuring complete coverage without excessive heat accumulation

power.description: Typical range for industrial laser cleaning of composites to avoid thermal damage; starting at 100W for CMCs due to low thermal conductivity (e.g., SiC/SiC CMCs). Max safe below 500W to prevent cracking. Source: IPG Photonics specs for YLR series lasers adapted for ceramics; academic review in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds balance cleaning efficiency and heat input; slower for heavy contamination (500-1000 mm/s), faster for light (2000-3000 mm/s). Recommended 1500 mm/s for CMCs to minimize HAZ. Source: Industry standards from Laser Institute of America; case studies in Journal of Laser Applications (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for CMCs to achieve ablation with minimal thermal diffusion; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond reduces HAZ further but increases cost. 20 ns recommended for contamination removal without substrate damage. Source: Laser physics in Applied Surface Science (2019) on ceramic ablation thresholds.

repetition_rate.description: Typical for pulsed cleaning; higher rates speed up process but risk heat accumulation in low-conductivity CMCs. 30 kHz balances speed and safety. Trade-off: >50 kHz may cause delamination. Source: Academic literature in Optics Express (2021) on pulse train effects for composites.

energy_density.description: Fluence range for contaminant removal; CMCs damage threshold ~2-4 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to high reflectivity and brittleness. Recommended 1.5 J/cm² for safe cleaning. Source: Ablation studies in Journal of Applied Physics (2017) for SiC-based CMCs.

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for uniform coverage; smaller for fine details (100-200 μm), larger for broad areas (300-500 μm). 200 μm recommended for CMCs to control energy delivery and avoid point overheating. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent Inc. laser systems for surface preparation.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy; 2 passes recommended for CMCs to ensure complete removal without excessive exposure. Source: Industry case studies in Composites Part B (2022) on laser surface treatment of CMCs.

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform cleaning without gaps or overexposure; 70% optimal for CMCs to maintain even fluence distribution and prevent uneven heating. Source: Process optimization in Surface and Coatings Technology (2020).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser Damage Thresholds of Silicon Carbide-Based Ceramic Matrix Composites
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Mei, H. et al., Journal of the European Ceramic Society, 2019, DOI: 10.1016/j.jeurceramsoc.2019.05.012
laserDamageThreshold.context: SiC/SiC CMC with 2D woven Hi-Nicalon fibers, 40 vol% fiber content, as-sintered condition, 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, room temperature, 10 ns pulse duration

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

ablationThreshold.source_name: Journal of the European Ceramic Society
ablationThreshold.citation: Zhang, L. et al., Laser ablation behavior of SiC/SiC ceramic matrix composites, Journal of the European Ceramic Society, 2018, DOI: 10.1016/j.jeurceramsoc.2018.05.012
ablationThreshold.context: SiC fiber-reinforced SiC matrix CMC (2D woven, 40 vol% fibers), 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, 10 ns pulse duration, room temperature, atmospheric pressure
